1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Direct Thai-government source (LDD), Thai ag-media (baanlaesuan), and FAO all confirm tropical year-round suitability and >25C optimum temperature.

2_climate.rationale: FAO and baanlaesuan both state >25C optimum and full sun; this aligns with Thai climate across all regions year-round.

3_soil.rationale: LDD covers dry-bed soil prep, baanlaesuan gives pH 5-7 range, FAO covers wet-land water levels; together they describe both cultivation modes.

4_water.rationale: FAO documents wet-land water depth progression (3-5 to 15-20 cm); baanlaesuan covers dry-bed irrigation; agkasetkaj covers microgreens watering.

5_planting.rationale: LDD gives Thai seeding rate (13-15 kg/rai); FAO gives wet-land vs dry-land spacing (40 cm vs 12 cm); baanlaesuan covers seed soaking; UF/IFAS covers propagation methods.

6_care.rationale: Care guidance kept high-level per safety policy: nitrogen fertilizer principle from LDD/baanlaesuan, mulching from baanlaesuan, crop rotation/sanitation from DOAE; specific NPK rates intentionally omitted.

7_pests_diseases.rationale: DOAE and OPSMOAC define white-rust biology and IPM; pajrmu provides quantitative Trichoderma efficacy data (16.25% to 5% disease incidence); LDD notes low pest pressure overall. Chemical doses stripped per policy.

8_harvest.rationale: LDD and baanlaesuan converge on 18-25 day pull-harvest for Chinese morning glory; FAO documents wet-land cut-and-come-again at 30 days then every 7-10 days; agkasetkaj covers microgreen harvest.

9_economics.rationale: LDD title and framing characterize morning glory as low-investment income-generating crop; agkasetkaj supports microgreens market context. No yield/profit guarantees per safety policy; ranges and qualitative framing only.

10_thailand_notes.rationale: KU Tha Chin study covers central-Thailand wet-cultivation context; pajrmu (Maha Sarakham) anchors the Northeast; Goethberg 2002 provides peer-reviewed heavy-metals food-safety context for Bangkok region. Framed as public-health context, not medical advice.

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: FAO is the classic global reference for cultivation systems; UF/IFAS provides extension-grade context including Florida invasive caveat; Wikipedia covers names and US regulatory framing. Section flags what does and does not transfer to Thailand.

overall_rationale: Six Thai-government / Thai-university / Thai-peer-reviewed sources plus three international sources cover both cultivar groups (Chinese vs water morning glory) and both cultivation modes (dry-bed vs wet-land). White-rust and heavy-metals risks are well-documented from primary research.
